key_points: Agriculture is one of the oldest economic activities and provides food, employment, and raw materials for industries
Agriculture depends on physical factors such as climate, soil, and relief, and human factors such as technology, irrigation, and labor
Subsistence farming focuses on producing crops for family consumption, while commercial farming focuses on producing crops for markets
Crop seasons in India include Kharif, Rabi, and Zaid, which depend on climate and rainfall patterns
Modern agriculture uses technology such as fertilizers, pesticides, and machines to increase productivity
examples: Kharif crops: Rice, maize, and cotton
Rabi crops: Wheat, barley, and mustard
Plantation crops: Tea in Assam and coffee in Karnataka
Cash crops used by industries: Cotton for textiles and sugarcane for sugar production
important_terms: Subsistence Farming: Farming where farmers grow crops mainly to feed their families
Commercial Farming: Farming where crops are grown for sale and profit
Crop Seasons: Different periods during which crops are grown such as Kharif, Rabi, and Zaid
Plantation Agriculture: Large-scale farming of a single crop such as tea, coffee, or rubber
Irrigation: The artificial supply of water to crops to improve agricultural production
exam_tips: Understand the difference between subsistence and commercial farming
Remember the crop seasons and examples of crops grown in each season
Learn the physical and human factors affecting agriculture
Know examples of food crops, cash crops, and plantation crops
Understand how modern technology has improved agricultural productivity
